title: Fabrica: Dual-Arm Assembly of General Multi-Part Objects via Integrated Planning and Learning
abstract: Multi-part assembly poses significant challenges for robotic systems to execute long-horizon, contact-rich manipulation with generalization across complex geometries. We present a dual-arm robotic system that unifies long-horizon planning and robust contact-rich control for assembling general multi-part objects directly from CAD models.
method_summary: Integrates task-and-motion planning with learned contact-rich manipulation policies for dual-arm robotic assembly directly from CAD models, achieving generalization across diverse object geometries. CoRL 2025 Best Paper.

title: Physics-Aware Combinatorial Assembly Sequence Planning using Data-free Action Masking
abstract: This paper studies assembly sequence planning (ASP) for physical combinatorial assembly. Given the shape of the desired object, the goal is to find a sequence of actions for placing unit primitives to construct the target shape while respecting physical stability constraints.
method_summary: Proposes a deep RL approach with physics-aware data-free action masking for combinatorial assembly sequence planning, ensuring physical feasibility without requiring simulation during training.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Applied to an Assembly Sequence Planning Problem with User Preferences
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has demonstrated its potential in solving complex manufacturing decision-making problems, especially in a context where the system learns over time with active human feedback. This paper applies DRL to assembly sequence planning incorporating user preferences.
method_summary: Applies deep RL to assembly sequence planning with human preference integration, enabling the system to learn user-preferred assembly strategies while satisfying feasibility constraints.

title: ASAP: Automated Sequence Planning for Complex Robotic Assembly with Physical Feasibility
abstract: ASAP: Automated Sequence Planning for Complex Robotic Assembly with Physical Feasibility. This work presents an automated approach to generating physically feasible assembly sequences for complex multi-part objects.
method_summary: Proposes ASAP, an automated assembly sequence planner that generates physically feasible plans for complex robotic assembly by combining geometric reasoning with learned feasibility prediction.

title: Optimal Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning (ORASP)
abstract: The optimal robotic assembly planning problem entails determining the sequence of actions for a robot to feasibly assemble a product from its components which minimizes a given objective. The problem is challenging due to the exponentially vast number of possible plans.
method_summary: Formulates optimal robotic assembly sequence planning as a combinatorial optimization problem and proposes efficient search algorithms with learned heuristics to find optimal plans.

title: Hierarchical Hybrid Learning for Long-Horizon Contact-Rich Robotic Assembly
abstract: Generalizable long-horizon robotic assembly requires reasoning at multiple levels of abstraction. While end-to-end imitation learning (IL) is a promising approach, it typically requires large amounts of demonstration data. This work proposes hierarchical hybrid learning combining planning with learned skills.
method_summary: Combines hierarchical task planning with learned contact-rich manipulation skills for long-horizon robotic assembly, achieving data efficiency through hybrid planning-learning architecture.

title: 3D Combinatorial Construction with Deep Reinforcement Learning
abstract: To address the challenges of 3D combinatorial construction, we propose a two-part deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework that tackles teaching the robot to understand the objective of an incomplete assembly and learning a construction policy.
method_summary: Proposes a two-part DRL framework for 3D combinatorial construction where one network understands assembly goals and another learns construction policies for sequential block placement.

title: REASSEMBLE: A Multimodal Dataset for Contact-rich Robotic Assembly and Disassembly
abstract: Built around the NIST Assembly Task Board 1 benchmark, REASSEMBLE includes four actions (pick, insert, remove, and place) involving 17 objects. The dataset contains 4,551 demonstrations, of which 4,035 are successful, with multimodal sensing data.
method_summary: Introduces a large-scale multimodal dataset for contact-rich robotic assembly/disassembly with 4,551 demonstrations across 17 objects, providing force, vision, and audio data for learning-based assembly.

title: Tolerance Allocation of Complex Systems Based on Supervised Machine Learning and Adaptive Sampling
venue: International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology
abstract: This paper presents a novel framework for the tolerance allocation of complex systems. The framework integrates adaptive sampling, importance sampling, and the C4.5 decision tree algorithm to enhance tolerance allocation efficiency and accuracy.
method_summary: Combines supervised ML (C4.5 decision trees) with adaptive sampling for efficient tolerance allocation in complex assemblies, reducing computational cost compared to Monte Carlo methods.

title: Genetic Algorithm Optimization Based on Manufacturing Prediction for an Efficient Tolerance Allocation Approach
abstract: The tolerance allocation is an extremely sensitive task due to the complex effects on quality, product, and cost. This paper proposes a GA optimization approach based on manufacturing prediction models for efficient tolerance allocation covering design and manufacturing aspects.
method_summary: Integrates ML-based manufacturing prediction models with genetic algorithm optimization for tolerance allocation, jointly optimizing quality and cost.

title: Statistical Tolerance Allocation Design Considering Form Errors Based on Rigid Assembly Simulation and Deep Q-Network
venue: International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology
abstract: In this paper, a statistical tolerance allocation method is presented that considers form errors. A combination of rigid assembly simulation and deep Q-network is used to optimize tolerance allocation for complex products.
method_summary: Applies Deep Q-Network reinforcement learning to statistical tolerance allocation considering form errors, using rigid assembly simulation for reward computation.

title: Learning to Generate Fine-Grained Robotic Assembly Instructions from Multi-View Images
abstract: We propose an end-to-end model known as the Neural Assembler. This model learns an object graph where each vertex represents recognized components from the images, and the edges specify assembly relationships and instructions.
method_summary: Proposes Neural Assembler, an end-to-end model that generates fine-grained assembly instructions from multi-view images by learning object graphs with component relationships.

title: Automatic Feature Recognition and Dimensional Attributes Extraction From CAD Models for Hybrid Additive-Subtractive Manufacturing
abstract: The Hierarchical Graph Convolutional Neural Network (HGCNN) model is implemented to accurately identify the composite additive-subtractive features within the synthetic CAD dataset. This enables automated process planning for hybrid manufacturing.
method_summary: Uses hierarchical graph CNNs to recognize manufacturing features in CAD models for hybrid additive-subtractive manufacturing, automating the feature recognition step of process planning.

title: BrepMFR: Enhancing Machining Feature Recognition in B-rep Models through Deep Learning and Domain Adaptation
abstract: We propose a novel deep learning network, BrepMFR, designed for Machining Feature Recognition (MFR) from Boundary Representation (B-rep) models. We transform the original B-rep model into a graph representation leveraging Transformer architecture and graph attention mechanism.
method_summary: Proposes BrepMFR using graph Transformers with attention mechanisms for machining feature recognition from B-Rep CAD models, with domain adaptation to bridge synthetic-to-real gaps.

title: Self-Supervised Masked BRep Autoencoders for Machining Feature Recognition
abstract: Machining feature recognition (MFR) is a technique that automatically extracts geometric shapes with specific machining semantics, such as holes, slots, bosses, and chamfers, from 3D CAD models. This work proposes self-supervised masked BRep autoencoders for pre-training representations useful for MFR.
method_summary: Introduces self-supervised pre-training via masked BRep autoencoders for machining feature recognition, learning transferable CAD representations without labeled data.

title: Transformer-Based B-rep Geometric Feature Recognition
abstract: We propose a Transformer-based approach for B-rep geometric feature recognition. In addition, we also proposed a dataset named Complex B-rep Feature Dataset (CBF), comprising 20,000 B-rep models covering more complex B-rep models aligned with industrial applications.
method_summary: Applies Transformer architecture to B-Rep geometric feature recognition with a new 20K-model dataset (CBF) covering industrially complex geometries beyond existing benchmarks.

title: BRepGAT: Graph Neural Network to Segment Machining Feature Faces in a B-rep Model
abstract: In recent years, there have been many studies using artificial intelligence to recognize machining features in three-dimensional models in the CAD/CAM domain. This work proposes BRepGAT, a graph attention network for segmenting machining feature faces in B-rep models.
method_summary: Proposes BRepGAT using graph attention networks for face-level segmentation of machining features in B-Rep CAD models, enabling fine-grained feature boundary identification.

title: Real-Time Tool-Path Planning Using Deep Learning for Subtractive Manufacturing
abstract: Tool-path planning is a crucial factor of computer-aided design (CAD) and computer-aided manufacturing (CAM). Previous path generation methods often transform the problem into local or global optimization. This work proposes a deep learning approach for real-time toolpath planning.
method_summary: Applies deep learning for real-time CNC toolpath planning, replacing iterative optimization with a learned model that generates near-optimal paths in milliseconds.

title: Data-driven Modelling of Machine Tool Feedrate Behavior with Neural Networks
abstract: Usually, Computer Aided Manufacturing (CAM) software estimates the machining times using the commanded feedrate from the toolpath file using basic kinematic settings. This paper proposes neural network models to predict actual machine tool feedrate behavior for more accurate machining time estimation.
method_summary: Trains neural networks to predict actual CNC machine feedrate behavior from toolpath data, improving machining time estimation accuracy over conventional CAM kinematic models.

title: Large Language Models for Manufacturing
abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive exploration of the integration of LLMs into the manufacturing domain, focusing on their potential to automate and enhance various aspects of manufacturing, from process planning to quality control and supply chain management.
method_summary: Surveys LLM applications in manufacturing including process planning automation, G-code generation, quality inspection, and supply chain optimization, identifying opportunities and hallucination risks.

title: Knowledge Graph Fusion with Large Language Models for Accurate, Explainable Manufacturing Process Planning
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) promise flexible, instruction-driven reasoning for tasks such as G-code synthesis and spindle-load queries, but they routinely hallucinate numeric values and provide no provenance. This paper fuses knowledge graphs with LLMs for manufacturing process planning.
method_summary: Fuses manufacturing knowledge graphs with LLMs to ground process planning in verified facts, reducing hallucination in G-code generation and machining parameter selection.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Cutting Parameter Dynamic Decision Method Considering Tool Wear for a Turning Machining Process
venue: International Journal of Precision Engineering and Manufacturing-Green Technology
abstract: Cutting parameter optimization is considered as an effective way for energy consumption saving. In the machining process, the tool wear of cutting tools varies with the rise of the number of workpieces processed. This paper proposes an RL-based dynamic cutting parameter decision method.
method_summary: Applies reinforcement learning to dynamically adjust cutting parameters during turning based on real-time tool wear state, optimizing energy consumption and surface quality simultaneously.

title: Making Informed Decisions in Cutting Tool Maintenance in Milling
abstract: Tool Condition Monitoring (TCM) is vital for maintaining productivity and product quality in machining. This study leverages machine learning to analyze real-time force signals collected from experimental milling operations for tool wear prediction and maintenance decision-making.
method_summary: Uses ML on real-time force signals for tool condition monitoring in milling, enabling data-driven maintenance decisions that balance tool life and part quality.

title: Implicit Neural Fields for Collision-Free Multi-Axis 3D Printing
abstract: This unified approach enables toolpath optimization across both surface and interior domains, allowing shell and infill paths to be generated via implicit field interpolation. The method uses implicit neural fields to represent collision-free multi-axis printing toolpaths.
method_summary: Represents multi-axis 3D printing toolpaths as implicit neural fields, enabling collision-free path optimization that unifies shell and infill generation for complex geometries.

title: GRACE: Efficient and Feasible Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning via Graph Representation Learning
abstract: Automatic Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning (RASP) can significantly improve productivity and resilience in modern manufacturing. With GRACE, we extract meaningful information from graph input and predict assembly sequences in a step-by-step manner using graph attention networks.
method_summary: Uses graph attention networks for robotic assembly sequence planning, learning to predict feasible assembly orders from part relationship graphs with physical constraint satisfaction.

title: Deep Neural Implicit Representation of Accessibility for Multi-Axis Manufacturing
abstract: One of the main concerns in design and process planning for multi-axis additive and subtractive manufacturing is collision avoidance between moving objects and stationary objects. We develop an implicit representation of the collision measure field via deep neural networks that can accurately interpolate the collision measure for arbitrary tool orientations.
method_summary: Learns implicit neural representations of manufacturing accessibility/collision fields for multi-axis machining, enabling rapid collision checking for arbitrary tool orientations without explicit geometric computation.

title: DeepMill: Neural Accessibility Learning for Subtractive Manufacturing
abstract: We introduce DeepMill, the first neural framework designed to accurately and efficiently predict inaccessible and occlusion regions under varying machining tool parameters, applicable to both 3-axis and 5-axis CNC machining configurations.
method_summary: First neural framework for predicting machining accessibility and occlusion regions for 3-axis and 5-axis CNC, replacing expensive geometric computation with learned predictions that generalize across tool parameters.

title: Implicit Neural Field-Based Process Planning for Multi-Axis Manufacturing
abstract: We present an implicit neural field-based framework for multi-axis process planning that overcomes limitations of discrete layer-based approaches by embedding both layer generation and toolpath design within a single differentiable implicit field representation using sinusoidally activated neural networks.
method_summary: Embeds multi-axis manufacturing process planning (layer generation + toolpath design) into a single differentiable implicit neural field, enabling continuous optimization of the entire manufacturing plan.

title: Connector-Aware General Robotic Assembly from Instruction Manuals via Vision-Language Models
abstract: Assembly hinges on reliably forming connections between parts; yet most robotic approaches plan assembly sequences and part poses while treating connectors as an afterthought. We propose a connector-aware assembly framework that uses vision-language models to parse instruction manuals and reason about connector types and mating constraints.
method_summary: Uses vision-language models to parse assembly instruction manuals and reason about connector types, enabling connector-aware robotic assembly planning that explicitly models mating constraints.

title: AssemMate: Graph-Based LLM for Robotic Assembly Assistance
abstract: This graph-based LLM enables knowledge graph question answering (KGQA), supporting human-robot interaction and assembly task planning for specific products. Beyond interactive QA, AssemMate also supports automated assembly sequence generation from product knowledge graphs.
method_summary: Combines knowledge graphs with LLMs for robotic assembly assistance, enabling natural language interaction for assembly planning and automated sequence generation from product structure knowledge.
